The joys of home ownership…

29 Nov 2017

“It wasn’t Putin who fashioned a Republican Party willing to tolerate something close to treason if it’s the price of corporate tax cuts. Even if all the Republicans in Congress read Harding’s book, they probably wouldn’t act. But at least they’d know what they’re abetting.” —Michelle Goldberg

28 Nov 2017

Thoughtful analysis of the difference between net neutrality and Title II status: “Any regulatory decision — indeed, any decision period — is about tradeoffs.” —Ben Thompson

28 Nov 2017

Six and a half years at Omni and I just committed my first use of semaphores.

wrong? What go could

27 Nov 2017

Swift by Northwest 2018 is coming to Portland next October! The first S×NW was amazing. Great talks, great community. From now till midnight December 1st, you can use the code “SwiftCyber” to save 25%.

27 Nov 2017

“Will a future generation look back … and wonder, mystifyingly, why a generation of humans believed in these platforms despite mounting evidence that they were tearing society apart” —Nick Bilton

27 Nov 2017

Rob from the poor; give to the rich… “By 2019, Americans earning less than $30,000 a year would be worse off under the Senate bill, CBO found. By 2021, Americans earning $40,000 or less would be net losers, and by 2027, most people earning less than $75,000 a year would be worse off.” —WaPo

27 Nov 2017

“Freedom of speech and freedom of religion do not exempt business owners from public accommodations laws, which require them to serve customers equally.” —John Corvino

27 Nov 2017

One of my shared links got retweeted into conservative^H reactionary twitter, so making good use of the block button today.

26 Nov 2017

Muahahahaha. I took a screen recording of the Quicken 2018 import progress sheet, found the account that was being imported during the crash (only visible for two frames of the recording), deleted the account from Quicken 2007, then import worked. Accounting vengeance is mine.

24 Nov 2017

Trying to import 25+ years of data from Quicken Mac 2007 into Quicken 2018. It crashes after importing most of the data. I’m the user the devs have nightmares about.

24 Nov 2017

This is so great. “The glasses are equipped with a camera, which feeds a video stream to a remote agent who then narrates what they see in real time over the phone for the [blind] user.” —Mary Hui

23 Nov 2017

Why GOP pols preach fear: “…if they had … just imagined being completely physically safe, the Republicans became significantly more liberal — their positions on social attitudes were much more like the Democratic respondents.” —John Bargh

23 Nov 2017

Happy Thanksgiving. This morning I’m thankful for coffee. Starting small…

23 Nov 2017

It’s true. Obama approves of oxygen. Now, let’s get these folks their fluorine. Can we kickstart that? Gofundme? twitter.com/scalzi/st…

22 Nov 2017

“Nevertheless, a majority of white voters backed a candidate who assured them that they will never have to share this country with people of color as equals. That is the reality that all Americans will have to deal with, and one that most of the country has yet to confront.” —Adam Serwer

21 Nov 2017

“In sum, the problem is not Trump’s advisers calling him names. The problem is that he’s unfit, his top advisers know it and collectively they are pretending that everything is fine.” —Jennifer Rubin

21 Nov 2017

Yet another step toward the rich and near-rich separating themselves from society. twitter.com/mateosfo/…

21 Nov 2017

“These days, [the Republicans] can’t even seem to get their fake story straight — and they literally start yelling obscenities when someone tries to point out the facts.” —Paul Krugman

20 Nov 2017

I need a giant cup of ability-to-focus today. Seattle gray is winning.
